Name: European Bee-eater
Best_seen_at: Gujarat and Rajasthan during passage migration
How_to_identify: Chestnut crown and mantle, yellow throat bounded by a black gorget, blue-green underparts. Central tail feathers are elongated. Similar to Blue-tailed Bee-eater but distinguished by the chestnut upperparts.
Diet: Bees, wasps, and other flying insects caught in flight

Name: Great Barbet
Habitat: Subtropical and tropical moist lowland and montane forests
Best_seen_at: Sattal (Uttarakhand), Great Himalayan National Park (Himachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Largest Indian barbet. Heavy yellow bill, violet-blue head, brown mantle, and yellowish-green belly with heavy blue/green streaking. Sexes are similar.
Diet: Fruits (especially figs), seeds, and occasional insects

Name: Brown-headed Barbet
Best_seen_at: Sultanpur National Park (Haryana), Keoladeo National Park (Rajasthan)
How_to_identify: Brown streaked head and breast, large orange-red bill, and a prominent yellow eye patch. Distinguished from Lineated Barbet by the orange-red orbital skin and heavier streaking.
Diet: Figs, drupes, berries, and occasionally insects

Name: Lineated Barbet
Habitat: Open forest, gardens, edge of cultivation
Best_seen_at: Jim Corbett National Park (Uttarakhand), Kaziranga National Park (Assam)
How_to_identify: Similar to Brown-headed Barbet but has a paler, flesh-colored bill and a pale yellow (not orange) eye patch. Streaking on head and breast is finer and cleaner.

Name: White-cheeked Barbet
Habitat: Gardens, forests, and wooded areas of Southern India
Best_seen_at: Thattekad Bird Sanctuary (Kerala), Bandipur National Park (Karnataka)
How_to_identify: Distinctive white supercilium and cheek patch bordered by a dark eye-stripe. Endemic to the Western Ghats and hilly regions of Southern India. Lacks the yellow eye patch of the Brown-headed Barbet.

Name: Golden-throated Barbet
Habitat: Subtropical and tropical moist montane forests
Best_seen_at: Eagle Nest Wildlife Sanctuary (Arunachal Pradesh), Neora Valley (West Bengal)
How_to_identify: Golden-yellow throat, crimson forehead patch, and blue cheeks. Green body. Distinguished from other montane barbets by the specific combination of golden throat and red forehead.

Name: Blue-throated Barbet
How_to_identify: Turquoise-blue throat and cheeks, red crown with a black band across the mid-crown, and red spots on the sides of the neck. Sexes are identical.

Name: Blue-eared Barbet
Best_seen_at: Kaziranga National Park (Assam), Namdapha National Park (Arunachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Small green barbet with blue ear-coverts and throat. Black band through the eye and red/yellow spots on the face. Smaller than the Blue-throated Barbet.

Name: Malabar Barbet
Best_seen_at: Western Ghats, Mollem National Park (Goa), Silent Valley (Kerala)
How_to_identify: Crimson forehead and throat, blue cheeks and throat-border. Smaller and more brightly colored than Coppersmith; lacks the yellow facial markings of the Coppersmith Barbet.

Name: Coppersmith Barbet
Habitat: Gardens, urban areas with trees, open woodlands
Best_seen_at: Common throughout India in urban and rural areas
How_to_identify: Crimson forehead and breast patch, yellow patches around the eyes and throat. Streaked green underparts. Known for its repetitive 'tonk-tonk' call. Sexes are similar.
Diet: Banyan, Peepal and other Ficus figs, berries, and occasional insects
